specific_location: N37.13817 W103.08806 (from GPS) This is barely outside the traditional Cottonwood Canyon 'campsite'. As I watched it moved east and entered Baca County.
description: Saw black bird fly to top of snag. Crest was immediately visible. Binoculars showed red eye. It called for awhile then stopped. I went to get my wife so she could see it. Also got camera. Came back and it was still in the same location. Got distant and then closer photos. When it next flew the white patches in the primaries were clearly seen. It moved several times but was going generally away from me and uphill so I could not get closer. I have seen the bird before in Arizona so it was not unfamiliar to me, just surprising.Behaviors: Bird stayed near top of trees calling softly. Seemed somewhat agitated but only I got fairly close. Moved from tree to tree. Would look around and call for awhile before moving to next tree. No additional birds were seen.Call: Soft series of notes with noticeable pausesPlumage: Adult
photographer: Photographer: Randy Siebert, Randy Siebert, Randy Siebert, Randy Siebert
